overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage showing a character riding a horse through a Mediterranean-style ancient landscape with stone aqueducts and a rural village. The clip transitions from riding alongside a large Roman-style construction site through open rocky terrain to a settlement with white stone buildings and wooden waterfront structures.

scene_state.functional_zone: ancient aqueduct construction site with wooden scaffolding

agents.ego_description: riding a horse along a dirt path beside a large stone aqueduct under construction with wooden scaffolding and crane mechanisms

entered.detail: large stone block wall with multiple arches forming an aqueduct structure
wooden construction scaffolding attached to the stone structure
large wooden crane mechanism with suspended platform hanging over the path
unpaved dirt trail with visible wheel ruts
rocky terrain and hills in the background

segments.scene_description: The segment opens with a third-person view following a horseback rider alongside a massive ancient stone aqueduct under construction. Wooden scaffolding and crane mechanisms line the left side while the path winds along the structure through semi-covered terrain.

events.description: exits the aqueduct construction area into open rocky landscape with full sky visibility

agents.ego_description: riding through open terrain with rocky hills on both sides and distant mountains visible ahead

entered.detail: sparse pine trees dotting the rocky landscape
mountain range visible in the far distance under blue sky

segments.scene_description: Leaving the aqueduct behind, the rider enters a wide-open rocky landscape with sparse vegetation and scattered pine trees. The terrain consists of dirt trails winding through light-colored rocky hills with distant mountains visible under a clear blue sky.

events.description: group of horse riders appears ahead on the path, moving in the same direction

agents.ego_description: riding along the path and approaching a group of other horse riders from behind

notable_agents.action: group of three riders on horseback traveling along the path ahead

entered.detail: cluster of white stone buildings and fortress-like structures becoming visible in the distance
distant section of the stone aqueduct visible on the left horizon

segments.scene_description: As the rider continues through the open terrain, a group of other horseback riders appears ahead on the path. In the distance, a village with white stone buildings and a distant aqueduct section becomes visible against the mountainous backdrop.

events.description: enters the village settlement with white stone buildings and wooden structures
approaches wooden dock structures and water canal on the right side

agents.ego_description: riding through the village past wooden scaffolding and dock structures, approaching the waterfront area

entered.detail: wooden platforms and docks with red banners along the water edge
body of water running alongside the path with wooden retaining walls

segments.scene_description: The rider enters a village settlement with prominent white stone fortress buildings and passes wooden dock structures adorned with red banners. The path runs alongside a water canal as the aqueduct remains visible in the distant left background.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement that transitions from a massive ancient construction site into a sprawling rocky wilderness and finally into a coastal village settlement. It begins by following a horseback rider alongside a towering stone aqueduct lined with wooden scaffolding and crane mechanisms. As the rider leaves the structure behind, the scene opens into a wide, sun-drenched landscape of light-colored rocky hills and scattered pine trees under a clear blue sky. The path then leads toward a group of distant riders as a village of white stone buildings emerges against a mountainous backdrop. Finally, the camera enters the settlement, passing wooden dock structures adorned with red banners and a water canal while the original aqueduct remains visible in the far distance.
